role: A React modal overlay that renders the operator's 'What needs you' work-queue of pending stack items and dispatches item selection to the shared decision host.
capabilities: Renders an overlay inbox panel driven by the decisions store
Lists pending stack items with loading, empty, error, and refresh-error states
Implements typed item dispatch with a fail-loud default for unknown stack kinds
Renders a decision-sequence preview row (name, meaning, recommendation, reversibility)
Opens a selected decision via a single CustomEvent('decision:open') path
Closes the inbox via Escape key, scrim click, or close button
Provides retry button to reload the decisions list
substance_why: The file implements a complete UI shell plus a real item renderer, error/empty/loading states, keyboard/scrim close, and retry wiring.
interactions_present: Click a decision row to dispatch decision:open with address, id, and fromInbox flag
Click retry button to call loadDecisions
interactions_missing: No focus trap or restored focus on open/close
No visible open trigger (assumed opened externally via store)
No handling for non-decision item types beyond an honest placeholder row
No actual decision resolution UI inside this file (delegated to the host)
No loading skeleton or virtualization for long lists
No scroll containment or aria-live announcements for dynamic states
performance_risks: Entire list re-renders on every store update without memoization
pick callback and renderStackItem recreated each render, causing every row to re-render
No virtualization if pending list grows large
reuse_verdict_why: Keep the inbox concept, store integration, and single-event open path, but rebuild the presentation, split renderers, and add focus/performance affordances.
decomposition_hints: Split overlay shell (scrim, header, close, aria) from list/body content
Extract item-type renderer registry so each stack kind has its own component file
Move DecisionRow to its own module
Separate data/loading orchestration from presentational markup
